channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia’s Migration Policies: Humanitarian Support and Temporary Visa Restrictions
item.description: Over the past 48 hours, Australia’s migration landscape has highlighted both the government’s humanitarian efforts and its strict border policies. The federal government granted refugee status to memb...

item.title: Historic Cape Dutch Farmhouse Restored as Community Hub in Cape Town
item.description: In Cape Town’s Glencairn Valley, the 19th-century Cape Dutch homestead known as Welcome Cottage has been revitalised into a lively community centre through a collaborative effort between the South Afr...

item.title: Fuel Shortage Causes Delays for Tanker Drivers Across Pakistan
item.description: Tanker drivers across Pakistan have reported significant delays at fuel depots due to a shortage of petrol, as the ongoing Middle East conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran disrupts shipping and d...

item.title: High Court Rules in Favor of Australian Designer Katie Perry in Trademark Dispute with Singer Katy Perry
item.description: Australian designer Katie Perry, who legally changed her surname to Taylor in 2015, has won a landmark High Court appeal in a long-standing trademark dispute with US pop star Katy Perry. The case revo...

item.title: Exim Bank Tanzania Honoured with Two Mastercard Awards for 2025
item.description: Exim Bank Tanzania has been recognised with two big Mastercard awards for 2025, showing strong financial growth and top-notch operations. The bank won for Best Elite Banking Proposition and Highest Ca...

item.title: Google Expands Gemini AI Integration in Chrome to India, Canada, and New Zealand
item.description: Google has expanded its Gemini AI integration in Chrome to new regions, including India, Canada, and New Zealand. The feature appears as a sidebar on desktop, allowing users to interact with Google’s ...

item.title: Escalating Iran-Israel Conflict: Saudi Missile Interceptions and Israeli Strikes on Beirut Reported
item.description: The Middle East conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the United States has intensified significantly. On March 11, 2026, Saudi Arabia reported intercepting seven ballistic missiles targeting an air ba...

item.title: Sowetan Morning Roundup: Insurance Murder Arrests, Johannesburg Property Fraud Cases, and Madlanga Commission Financial Links
item.description: This Sowetan morning briefing, published on 11 March 2026, highlights three major stories making headlines in South Africa. Firstly, recent arrests have expanded the investigation into an alleged R10 ...

item.title: Reza Pahlavi Urges Iranians to Prioritize Safety Over Protests Amid US-Israel-Iran Conflict
item.description: In the midst of escalating conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran in early 2026, Reza Pahlavi, the son of Iran's last shah, issued a public statement advising Iranians to remain indoors ...

item.title: Florida Woman Charged with Attempted Murder After Shooting at Rihanna's Los Angeles Home
item.description: On March 8, 2026, police for Los Angeles arrest one 35-year-old woman from Orlando, Florida, named Ivanna Lisette Ortiz, afta she allegedly fire about 10 shots from one white Tesla outside Rihanna Bev...

item.title: Ways Instagram Creators Can Earn From 1 Million Views
item.description: Instagram does not directly pay users for views like YouTube. Even if your content hits 1 million views, you won’t automatically earn cash from the platform. However, there are several ways Kenyan con...

item.title: National Assembly implements salary cuts and austerity measures for energy and cost saving
item.description: In Islamabad, the National Assembly Secretariat has taken strong steps for austerity and energy conservation to support the government’s national austerity campaign. The salaries of Members of the Nat...

item.title: Meghan Markle to headline women’s retreat in Sydney with premium tickets
item.description: Meghan Markle, the Duchess of Sussex, is set to be the main attraction at a three-day women’s retreat in Sydney this April. Organised by Her Best Life, a community group founded by Gemma O’Neill, the ...

item.title: UK Government Bans Al Quds Day March in London Over Public Order Concerns
item.description: The UK government has authorised the Metropolitan Police's request to ban the Al Quds Day march scheduled for London, citing concerns about 'serious public disorder'. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood em...

item.title: CAF Increases Prize Money for African Club Competitions
item.description: CAF has announced a major boost in prize money for Africa's top club football competitions this season. The Champions League winners for 2025-2026 will get $6 million, a 50 percent increase, while the...

item.title: Philippine Government Prepares Continuous Support for Filipinos Affected by Middle East Conflict
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) confirmed on Wednesday that the Philippine government is fully prepared to assist Filipinos affected by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East....

item.title: Court Returns Mickey Rourke's Los Angeles Home to Landlord After Eviction
item.description: Actor Mickey Rourke has officially lost possession of his rented home in Los Angeles after a court ruling granted control of the property back to the landlord, Eric T. Goldie. According to Los Angeles...

item.title: Sault Police Look Into Alleged Hate-Motivated Attack
item.description: Sault Ste. Marie police are investigating an incident that may have been motivated by hate. Authorities have not yet confirmed the specifics of the assault, including the identity of the alleged victi...

item.title: FG Evacuates Nigerians from Iran Safely Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The Federal Government don start the process to evacuate Nigerians wey dey Iran wey wan return home because the situation for that side don dey tense. Na Abike Dabiri-Erewa, Chairman and CEO of Nigeri...

item.title: Review and analysis of Shahrnush Parsipur's Women Without Men: feminist themes and historical context in a new English translation
item.description: Shahrnush Parsipur's novella Women Without Men, originally written in the late 1970s and published in 1989, has recently been made available in English for the first time, translated by Faridoun Farro...
